steps.title: Locate the causal statement
steps.content: The passage opens with a direct causal claim: “We are creatures of the sun. We love light and life. That is why we crowd into the towns and cities.” This statement links human nature to urban settlement.

steps.title: Match the motivation to the choices
steps.content:
Choice A identifies those exact psychological drivers: being creatures of light and needing human connection motivate people to cities.

The other three either reverse the passage's logic (B claims isolation in cities, C says country is preferred) or miss the causal point entirely (D).

That matches choice A — A is correct.
